subject: Holiday carry over — can I use it in April?
message: Hi, I didn't manage to use all my leave last year and carried over 4 days. I want to book a week off in early April — is that okay or does the carry over have to be used before April? I've already got 3 days booked for the bank holidays.
resolution: Advised that carry-over must be used by 31 March. Priya had until the end of March to book it or it would lapse. She confirmed she'd book something.

subject: Change bank account for salary
message: Hi, I've changed banks and need to update where my salary goes. My new sort code is 20-45-67 and account number is 83920174. Please can you update this before the end of month payroll run? My old account will be closed on 28th Feb. Thanks
resolution: Passed to payroll team. Updated in time for Feb payroll. Confirmed with Tom by email.

subject: How much leave do I have left?
message: Hi, I'm trying to plan a holiday in June but I'm not sure how much leave I have left to use. I've taken 8 days so far this year. Can you tell me my current balance? I think I'm on the standard 25-day entitlement.
resolution: Checked in Rippling — Aisha had 17 days remaining (25 total, 8 taken). Replied with balance and confirmed no carry-over from last year.

subject: Dentist appointment tomorrow — how does this work?
message: I've got a dentist appointment tomorrow at 11am, should take about an hour. Do I need to take it as annual leave or sick leave? My manager (Rory MacDonald) is on holiday so I'm not sure who to ask.
resolution: Advised James to use the time informally and make it up, or take as annual leave — short appointments are at manager discretion. Suggested he flag it to Rory on return. Not recorded as sick leave.

subject: Paternity leave — when do I need to give notice?
message: Hi, my partner is due in late May and I want to make sure I get paternity leave sorted properly. I've been at Paluza for about 18 months. Do I qualify for the enhanced pay? And when do I need to let you know? Want to plan ahead.
resolution: Confirmed Marcus is eligible (over 26 weeks service). Advised to notify People Ops by 15th week before EWC — which would be early February. He was slightly late but we accepted the notification given the circumstances. Sent him the paternity leave form.

subject: Parking permit application
message: Hi, I've recently moved and now commute in by car. Can I apply for one of the car park permits? My new address is 14 Clifton Road, Bristol, BS8 1AF. I drive a dark blue Volkswagen Golf, reg BK21 YTR. Happy to go on the waiting list if there's one.
resolution: Added Sophie to the parking permit waiting list. Sent confirmation with expected wait time (~6 weeks). Noted vehicle details in the facilities system.

subject: Notice period question
message: Hi, I'm just exploring options at the moment but wanted to check — what's my notice period? I joined in March 2022 so I think I'm coming up on 2 years. Does that change anything? Not made any decisions yet, just want to understand where I stand.
resolution: Advised Connor he's currently on 1-month notice (under 2 years) and that it increases to 6 weeks at his 2-year anniversary in March. He thanked us, no further action.

subject: Sick note — returning after chest infection
message: Hi, I've been off for 10 days with a chest infection. My GP has given me a fit note. I'm due back on Monday. Do I need to do anything else — like a return to work form? First time I've been off this long so not sure of the process.
resolution: Advised Nadia to submit the fit note via Rippling and that her manager would do a brief return-to-work conversation on her first day back. Reminded her the conversation is informal and supportive. She confirmed she'd uploaded the fit note.

subject: Cycle to work scheme — how do I sign up?
message: Hi, I want to get a new bike and was told we do a cycle to work scheme. How does it work and when can I join? Is there a maximum amount? My salary is £38,500 so I wasn't sure if there's a cap based on that.
resolution: Sent Daniel the cycle to work scheme info pack. Cap is £3,000. Next sign-up window opens 1 April. Added to the notification list.

subject: Holiday during notice period
message: I've handed in my notice (last day will be 12 April). I have 6 days of annual leave remaining. My manager has asked me to work as normal through my notice — but can I take the leave? Or will it just be paid out at the end?
resolution: Advised Yuki that the company can require leave to be taken during notice or pay it out at the end. Spoke to her manager (Fatima Al-Hassan) who agreed to let Yuki take the 6 days from 7–12 April as her final working period.

subject: Flexible working request — 4-day week
message: Hi, I'd like to formally request to move to a 4-day week (Monday–Thursday) from June. I've been in my role for 2 years and have spoken informally with my manager who is open to it. What's the formal process? Do I need to submit anything specific?
resolution: Sent Rebecca the flexible working request form and explained the 3-month process. Confirmed she meets the eligibility criteria (26 weeks' service). She submitted the form 22 March.

subject: P60 — when will I get it?
message: Hi, I need my P60 for a mortgage application. When does it normally come out? My application deadline is 30 April and I want to make sure I have it in time.
resolution: Advised Kwame P60s are issued by 31 May each year (statutory deadline). As his deadline was 30 April, suggested requesting a letter of employment with salary details from People Ops as an interim. Sent the letter same day.

subject: Three sick absences — will I get a formal warning?
message: I realised I've had 3 separate periods of sickness in the last 12 months (a cold in October, a stomach bug in December, and 2 days last week). I've seen the policy mentions a trigger at 3 occurrences. Does this automatically lead to a disciplinary? I'm a bit worried.
resolution: Reassured Mei-Ling that the trigger doesn't mean disciplinary action — it prompts a supportive conversation with her manager. Suggested she proactively mention it to her manager (James Hartley) to avoid it feeling unexpected. She seemed relieved.

subject: Team member leave clashing — what's the rule?
message: Hi, I've got two people on my team (Aisha Okonkwo and James Hartley) both requesting the same week off in August. It's a busy period and I can only approve one. Is there an official policy on how to handle clashes? First come first served?
resolution: Confirmed no strict policy — manager discretion applies, with first-come-first-served as a reasonable default. Suggested Rory document his decision and communicate it clearly to the team to avoid disputes.

subject: Probation review for new starter
message: Hi, I manage a new joiner (Liam Boyd, started 14 January) and his 3-month review is coming up. He's doing well and I want to confirm him as permanent. Is there a form I need to fill in? Does he need to be present for this?
resolution: Sent Fatima the probation completion form in Rippling. Confirmed the employee doesn't need to sign off separately — manager completes it and the employee gets an automated confirmation email. Completed 8 April.

subject: Working from Portugal for 3 weeks
message: Hi again! I'm planning to visit my family in Portugal for three weeks in July and would like to work remotely from there. Is this allowed? I don't think I'll need to do anything special for 3 weeks but wanted to check first.
resolution: Advised that short-term working abroad requires approval and a quick check on tax/social security implications. Flagged to our payroll adviser — confirmed 3 weeks in Portugal is generally fine with no tax trigger. Approved with line manager sign-off. Priya informed.

subject: April payslip seems wrong — short by about £200
message: Hi, my April payslip is lower than usual by about £200. I started the cycle to work scheme this month — could that be it? Just want to make sure it's right. My usual take-home is around £2,450.
resolution: Confirmed the £200 deduction is the first cycle to work instalment (he signed up for a £1,200 bike over 12 months = £100/month... checked with payroll — actually was set up as £200 this month due to a catch-up from the April window start). Payroll corrected to £100/month from May. April difference refunded in May payroll.

subject: Buy extra holiday — missed the window?
message: Hi, I completely forgot to sign up to buy extra holiday days during the March window. Is there any way to do it now? I'd like to buy 3 extra days for a longer summer holiday.
resolution: Unfortunately the buy/sell window had closed. Advised Daniel the next window opens March next year. Suggested he check his remaining entitlement and whether he could spread existing leave to accommodate the summer trip.

subject: Requesting an employment reference
message: Hi, I'm applying for a mortgage and the lender needs an employment reference confirming my start date, job title, and salary. Can you provide this? They need it on headed paper.
resolution: Issued an employment reference letter on company headed paper confirming: start date (14 August 2022), role (Product Analyst), and annual salary (£42,000). Sent to Sophie as a PDF to forward to her lender.

subject: Sick pay while on paternity leave — sprained my ankle
message: Hi, a bit of an odd one — I'm currently on paternity leave and I've sprained my ankle quite badly (happened at the weekend, nothing to do with work). Do I need to report this as sick leave? Will it affect my paternity pay? Probably overthinking it but wanted to check.
